phone_presence_notes: Jespah's tone was genuinely warm, he offered unsolicited helpful advice on storage unit search (SpareFoot.com) and sizing, the customer responded positively and said 'I appreciate it' — rapport felt natural rather than scripted.
top_strength: Exceptional customer service instinct — proactively helped Brian find a storage unit with a specific website recommendation and unit sizing guidance, going above the transaction to solve the customer's actual problem.
coaching_note: Save Your Ass, Meet Your Mover, and On-Time Guarantee were all missed — the three key differentiators were not mentioned at any point. Clock mechanics (minimum, prorate) were vague and incomplete. Customer name was only used twice — one short of target. These are structural gaps. The natural rapport and helpful instincts are a real asset; now layer the full framework on top of that foundation. Also: note Brian — not Ryan — at the end of the call (rep called customer the wrong name).
